title: Caleana sullivanii
taxonomy: Caleana sullivanii is a species of orchid native to Australia. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. Within the Orchidaceae family, Caleana sullivanii is classified under the subfamily Orchidoideae and tribe Diurideae. The genus Caleana is part of the orchid subfamily Orchidoideae, which includes about 300 species. Further classification of Caleana sullivanii places it in the family Orchidaceae, subfamily Orchidoideae, tribe Diurideae, and the genus Caleana.

intro: Caleana sullivanii, commonly known as the Australian Christmas Bell, is a species of terrestrial orchid found in eastern Australia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as colds and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Caleana sullivanii include phenanthrenes, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Caleana sullivanii is commonly prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine as a treatment for various health issues.
